All right, this next part of the course, I want to actually show you what an email nurturing campaign looks like. And I'm going to let you behind the scenes on a story brand chain of emails that we sent that really built our business. I'm going to show you each email and why they were constructed, the way they were constructed, and just a grand sort of flyover so that as you create great email campaigns, you can kind of compare and contrast what we've done. But I'll tell you what I was thinking with each email. Well, the first thing that happens, this is actually inside of Infusionsoft. This is an Infusionsoft map of a bunch of emails that we send out that we send them about two or three days apart, and we can program them to go whenever we want them to go. You will want to work with an Infusionsoft expert, or if you're an Infusionsoft person, you understand how to do this. But you'll basically want to stay in your emails, okay, wait two days, and then send the next one. Wait two days, and then send the next one, or something like that. And then you can start to get the hang of it as you start working with coders and programmers who understand this stuff. You can actually create this campaign yourself in MailChimp. It's not very difficult. It'll take you a couple of YouTube videos and maybe a short afternoon to figure out how to do it yourself. So don't get overwhelmed with the tech of it. It can get as complicated or as simple as you want. I'll just show you an overview of why we created the emails the way we did. The first thing that happens is somebody gets into our system, and so they download a lead generating PDF. We have their email address, and then we're actually going to, at 8am the next day, send them an email. Now this email is called Covert Conversation with a Fan. This is an email where I just sent something a little bit personal, a little bit, hey, let's get to know each other. It's not a hard sell. I'm really not selling much of anything. It's just a story about how I actually encountered a customer of mine and had a conversation with them about my product without them knowing who I was and realized they didn't have a way of talking about it. They were just using a bunch of language that was confusing, and I realized I needed to clarify my message. Now what's the specific reason to send that email? I want a positive pain point. I want them to say, man, I need to clarify my message too, and that's it. I'm literally going to leave it at that. Then I wait two days and I send them another email. Now this is an old email campaign. It was back during the Republican primaries, and I sent a video explaining why I thought Trump was doing well in the Republican primaries because he had a clear message. He might not have been as good of a candidate or experienced of a candidate, really didn't know what he was going to do, but his message was clear than any of the other candidates. That got a lot of attention. Even people who didn't like Trump couldn't help but agree, this guy's message is pretty darn clear. Then we keep going, wait a couple days, and I've got a paradigm shift email. The paradigm shift email basically said, don't play the hero in your story, let your customer be the hero. You can see we're getting deeper into the content the further the email goes, and it also hits another pain point. They now know I've got to clarify my message. I took two emails to just hit that pain point and show a success story, Trump, who's winning, whether you want him to or not, because he's got a clear message. That means if you want to win, you've got to have a clear message. Then that third email is, now let's talk about how to create a clear message. One of the things you want to do is never play the hero because that's going to be confusing to your customer. That's the paradigm shift email. Then I wait two days and I've got, it's time to communicate clearly. Now I'm actually starting to call them to action. I've opened up my product, my online course, and it's time for them to buy that course. It's a much clearer call to action. Now the course is available and open to them, and I'm going to overcome some objections. Will Storybrand work for you? The number one question we get at Storybrand is, yeah, it sounds great, but you know, I'm a dentist, so it probably won't work for me. Well, we've had 50 dentists come through. It works for dentists, plumbers, everybody. But when we realized that's a question everybody was asking of course, we want to overcome that objection or that hesitance in an email. So this email, I'm overcoming an objection. I wait one day and then they get a free video. It's a piece of the product, the online course, explaining why customers actually do buy, gives them a taste, makes them want more, helps them realize there's real value in this thing, and I want more of that value. I wait two more days and I have an entertaining email that it's just basically about how Star Wars and Tommy Boy are the same movie. But the hidden agenda behind that email is to say there are formulas for creating stories, formulas capture the human brain, they capture attention, and if you want to capture attention for your brand, you've got to know these formulas. Again, I'm saying you need this course, but it's an entertaining way, a soft way of explaining that. I wait one day. This next email is just an email that presents a test to the customer. I call it plumber to Starbucks, right? What I'm saying is in this email, if you're a business owner, go to Starbucks, find a random person. I don't care if they're a plumber or whoever. Have them look at your website for five seconds and see if they can tell you what you offer, how it's going to make their life better, and what they need to do to buy it. Well, probably nobody getting that email is actually going to pick up their laptop and walk down to Starbucks, but they're all going to do it in their head. And when they do it in their head, they're going to realize that plumber looking at their website has no clue what they offer. That's a test. It's a way of saying to potential customers, you know what? I would fail that test, which means I need this product. So a test, presenting a test to a customer in an email is another terrific way to create an email for your email sequence. Then I wait a day and I've got a testimonial. Now I've got a video with somebody who's taken the course and quadrupled, yes, quadrupled their revenue after they took the course. So people are saying, wait a second, this is working for other people who are just like me. I wait one more day and I say today is your last day to get the product. I tend to get most sales on the last day, but it's the last day for you to get the product. Then I just wait a few more hours. I send them a second email on the last day that says, guys, this is it. You're running out of time. I wait another few hours and I send them a final email and after this they can't get the product anymore. That is a simple campaign. It's honestly not super creative, but it did take a lot of time to build and each email was gone over meticulously. Thinking about how the customer would interact with this email, how they would read it and whether or not they would respond to it. I also want to go back and say, well, the customer doesn't know everything about my product, so I've got to go all the way back and kind of bridge the curse of knowledge and start at the beginning and slowly on ramp them to where they want to buy this product. You want to think about this email nurturing campaign, not unlike a dating relationship. You just want to make a positive first impression, make the person feel good about themselves. A week later you might see them again and there's some second, third, fourth, fifth steps. If you try to ask them to marry you on that first date, they're not going to. The great thing about email nurturing campaigns, or this is an email sales campaign, is it allows you to do that slowly over time. Now that you know how to write a great sales letter and you know how to put together a series of emails, this is an example of the finished product. What you're actually looking to do is create a finished email nurturing or sales campaign that gets results for you or your client. Hope you learned a lot from this terrific example. It's worked great for us and I know it will work great for you.
